comment: Cost-per-flare comparison: inhaled mRNA-IL-1Ra at scale vs canakinumab.

cost_ratio_canakinumab_to_inhaled_mrna.interpretation: At central inhaled-mRNA economics ($18-180/yr) the cost ratio vs canakinumab ($100K-300K/yr) is 500-3000x. Even the conservative high-cost low-scale inhaled scenario ($1200/yr at 10 flares) is 50-250x cheaper than canakinumab.

vs_compounded_anakinra: Anakinra itself is approximately $1500-3000/month off-label retail = $18-36K/year. Inhaled mRNA-IL-1Ra at scale beats this by another 100-1000x at high-volume scenario and remains competitive (or breaks even) at low-volume specialty pricing. The competitive economic case is strongest against canakinumab, but the case vs anakinra is patient-experience-driven (no SC injection at flare onset) rather than purely cost.
